interactions.summary: A student asks why a non-stupid school board does not change the system; Jiang answers that board members, presidents, and vice presidents are friends who control power and steal together.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Student question followed by Jiang answer.

interactions.summary: Jiang extends the corruption model from universities to Canada, US government, regulation, and the military, arguing that management expands while real work declines.

speaker_notes.note: This is a performed student question and Jiang answers directly in segment 0034.

claims.claim: Jiang says corrupt university boards do not reform because the board, president, and vice president are friends who control power and can protect each other.
In Jiang's Stratford University example, administrators extracted salaries, expenses, loans, and insider payments even as the institution went bankrupt.
Jiang predicts that many US universities will face similar bankruptcies over the next five to ten years because top managers are extracting together.
claims.temporal_scope: Prediction made on 2025-10-11 about the next five to ten years.

claims.claim: He argues that the same bureaucratic pattern appears beyond universities: Canada, US government, regulation, and the military all show management growth over real work.
Jiang contrasts four-star generals' personal planes with veterans on food stamps to argue that military bureaucracy rewards commanders while soldiers lack basics.

signature_moments.moment: If you steal alone you might get caught; if you steal together you become untouchable.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you steal together then you're untouchable
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains corruption as network behavior, not just individual greed.

signature_moments.moment: The general has a private jet; the veteran needs food stamps.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Every four-star general has this plane
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It condenses the military-bureaucracy critique into a stark allocation image.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's implied model of boards, presidents, vice presidents, and trustees protecting one another while extracting from an institution.

chronology_notes.note: Contains a dated prediction from 2025-10-11 that many American universities may go bankrupt over the following five to ten years.

uncertainty_notes.note: Some ASR around 'dummy' and the Stratford setup is noisy; use the broader answer rather than the corrupted phrase.
